title: Neuro-Symbolic Drive: Rule-Grounded Faithful Reasoning for Driving VLAs
author: Xiangbo Gao, Xiukun Huang, Boyu Lu, Junge Zhang, Mengjie Mao, Jiachen Li, Wei Xiong, Zhengzhong Tu
raw_description: arXiv:2606.23938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Driving VLA models incorporating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning are attractive because they leverage pretrained VLM representations and expose intermediate decisions in natural language, yet current rationales often lack the step-by-step decision semantics needed to keep the rationale causally connected to the planned motion. We introduce Neuro-Symbolic Drive, a neuro-symbolic driving framework that supervises a driving VLA with rule-grounded reasoning traces extracted directly from classical rule-based planners. Our key observation is that rule-based planners are symbolic AI systems that already function as executable reasoning engines: they reason about active safety constraints, search over candidate maneuvers, and select a final trajectory. We instrument these planners in simulation to capture both the executed trajectory and the internal decision trace at each rule-evaluation step. Each trace is serialized into structured rule-grou
summary: 提出神经符号驱动框架，通过规则基规划器提取规则基础推理轨迹，监督驾驶VLA模型，实现因果一致的逐步决策推理。
analysis_note: 创新性地结合神经符号与驾驶VLA，提升推理可解释性，但应用场景较窄。
